scope: Inventory frozen runtime laws and define the seeded local-Docker integration scenario matrix. No runtime implementation is introduced by this artifact.

law_classifications.title: ActivityPlan remains pure graph-diff output
law_classifications.behavior: GraphDiff compiles to ActivityPlan without persistence, Docker, HTTP, filesystem, or product-specific interpretation.
negative_cases: planning must reject stale current/desired graph evidence
planning must not import Docker/runtime interpreters
planning must not treat observations as desired topology
law_classifications.handoff: #871 consumes this by defining what additional pinned material the realization adapter needs beyond PlannedActivity.

law_classifications.title: Coordinator records durable intent, commits, then calls the adapter
law_classifications.behavior: One coordinator command advances at most max_effects ready activities by journaling STEP_STARTED before effect execution and journaling a terminal step result afterward.
negative_cases: no UnitOfWork or Postgres lock may remain active while an adapter executes
an adapter exception becomes explicit uncertainty, not an erased failure
completed replay must not repeat an already recorded effect
law_classifications.handoff: #874 must preserve this transaction shape when the adapter becomes real Docker realization.

law_classifications.title: Execution uses admitted plan truth, not current mutable graph truth
law_classifications.behavior: Realization material must be derived from the exact graph/plan admitted for the run; later workspace graph pointer movement cannot retarget work.
negative_cases: workspace mismatch fails before durable intent
graph drift after approval/admission does not change runtime targets
adapters must not load current graph truth themselves
law_classifications.handoff: #871 should introduce or name the pinned realization context; #872/#873 should use it for Docker material.

law_classifications.title: Docker mutation requires proven ownership
law_classifications.behavior: The Docker interpreter may mutate or remove only resources with matching CPK ownership fingerprints; stop and remove remain distinct operations.
negative_cases: foreign container identity must fail before mutation
retained data must not be removed as compute cleanup
stop-node must not imply remove-node-resource
law_classifications.handoff: #872 must define owned local Docker naming/label/fingerprint proof for seeded products.

law_classifications.title: Secret values are resolved only at effect boundary
law_classifications.behavior: Product descriptors may declare secret delivery requirements, but actual secret values must not enter descriptors, logs, events, errors, argv evidence, or public environment.
negative_cases: POSTGRES_PASSWORD must not appear in public_environment
Docker command evidence must not include secret values
secret-free products must not gain implicit secret channels
law_classifications.handoff: #872 can use a bounded test secret resolver for local Docker; generalized secret management remains outside ACTIVITY unless an issue explicitly scopes it.

law_classifications.title: Socket edges drive runtime dependency bindings
law_classifications.behavior: Requirement/provider socket connections in the desired graph determine runtime environment bindings and endpoint references for containers.
negative_cases: missing required provider binding fails before container start
incompatible protocol/socket bindings fail at graph validation or materialization
Docker-local endpoint synthesis must not rewrite graph truth
law_classifications.handoff: #873 owns URL/connection-string synthesis for hello, router, multiplexer, cpk-server, and postgres descriptor edges.

law_classifications.title: Container start and application readiness are distinct
law_classifications.behavior: Starting compute proves only process/container creation; health/readiness requires separate verification checks and observations.
negative_cases: process start must not mark WaitForHealthy as satisfied
TCP reachability must not imply HTTP or Postgres verification success
failed readiness blocks current graph advancement
law_classifications.handoff: #872 can start containers; #874 records observations; #875 advances only after accepted success.

law_classifications.title: Observed runtime state extends read truth without mutating desired graph
law_classifications.behavior: Runtime observations are durable evidence and projections; they do not become the desired graph or silently advance the current graph pointer.
negative_cases: observed endpoint data must be bounded/redacted where required
failed or uncertain observations block advancement
current graph compare-and-set must reject stale base truth
law_classifications.handoff: #874 persists observations; #875 performs accepted current graph advancement.

law_classifications.title: Approved plan review gates execution admission
law_classifications.behavior: The operator requests a plan, a manager reviews and approves/rejects it, and admission accepts only current sufficient approval evidence for that exact plan/session.
negative_cases: missing, rejected, stale, insufficient-scope, or different-plan approval fails closed
acceptance paths must not insert approval rows directly except store-primitive tests
approval queue read/detail surfaces must show enough plan/risk evidence for review
law_classifications.handoff: #880/#881 fill the approval queue/detail public workflow before cpk-server route acceptance.

law_classifications.title: Acceptance uses immutable OCI coordinates, not local tags
law_classifications.behavior: Seeded product descriptors pin image digest/provenance, and published-image smokes pull by digest with local rebuild disabled.
negative_cases: a local tag is development evidence only
descriptor digest and catalogue checksum must change after republishing cpk-server backend/runtime behavior
acceptance cannot claim published proof from a local build
law_classifications.handoff: #878 owns cpk-server republish after runtime behavior enters the server backend.

law_classifications.title: Remote control access points are explicit future work
law_classifications.behavior: ACTIVITY may verify via Docker-local networking; public CPK control ingress, Cloudflare portals, and remote over-the-wire control dispatch remain future topology/runtime work.
negative_cases: do not add portal URLs as magic cpk-server node properties
do not require arbitrary product servers to expose CPK wrapper routes in ACTIVITY
do not implement AWS/GCP/Kubernetes runtime portals in local Docker activity realization
law_classifications.handoff: #879 closeout should explicitly point remote control communication back to #882 and later runtime extraction.

seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.title: Initial deployment of cpk-server backed by Postgres descriptors
expected_proof: containers start on an owned Docker network
Postgres password is delivered as secret environment only
cpk-server readiness succeeds through HTTP provider socket
current graph advances after accepted successful realization
known_gaps: Postgres retained data volume ownership and cleanup policy must be implemented in Docker interpreter
connection-string synthesis from provider socket to cpk-server env bindings belongs to #873

seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.title: Initial deployment of standalone hello-server
expected_proof: published hello-server image is pulled by digest
HELLO_MESSAGE and HELLO_DEPENDENCIES_JSON public env are applied
HTTP live/ready checks pass on the internal provider socket
known_gaps: dynamic per-instance dependency declarations are not part of the base descriptor yet

seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.title: Hello server with an HTTP dependency edge
expected_proof: graph edge is materialized as HELLO_DEPENDENCIES_JSON for the dependent node
dependent hello can perform an HTTP call to its provider over Docker-local networking
known_gaps: hello-server descriptor currently has no explicit requirement socket; #873 must decide whether per-instance product parameterization can add the requirement without changing base product truth

seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.title: HTTP active router forwards to hello-server target
seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.graph_transition: EmptyGraph -> desired graph, then update target if supported
expected_proof: router target environment/control parameter is derived from graph edge
external smoke request to router receives hello response
target switch is represented as graph change and activity plan work, not observed-state rewrite
known_gaps: router target binding details must be read from product descriptor/current server tests in #873
runtime mutation/control route is not in ACTIVITY unless already descriptor-supported by local Docker parameters

seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.title: HTTP multiplexer primary response with observer delivery
expected_proof: MULTIPLEXER_PRIMARY_URL is required and bound from graph edge
MULTIPLEXER_OBSERVER_A_URL is optional and bound when present
primary response drives client-visible result while observer delivery remains process behavior
known_gaps: observer delivery evidence is product smoke/integration evidence, not durable CPK observation unless #874 explicitly records it

seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.title: Teardown removes owned compute while preserving retained Postgres data
expected_proof: owned compute is stopped/removed through explicit activities
retained postgres-data volume is not removed by configuration/compute cleanup
current graph advances to EmptyGraph only after accepted teardown result
known_gaps: retained data ownership fingerprints must be distinct from ephemeral compute cleanup fingerprints

seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.title: cpk-server HTTP/MCP workflow exercises same operations services
seeded_integration_scenario_matrix.graph_transition: operator-created desired graph -> admitted run -> local Docker realization
edges: scenario-specific edges from the cases above
expected_proof: operator imports product descriptors
operator starts session, sets desired graph, requests plan, requests approval
manager lists pending approval and decides
operator admits, claims, executes, reads final state through HTTP and MCP
known_gaps: published cpk-server image must be republished in #878 after backend runtime behavior changes

product_parameterization_gaps.evidence: Base hello-server descriptor has no requirement socket and ships HELLO_DEPENDENCIES_JSON=[] as public env.
product_parameterization_gaps.impact: A dependent hello scenario needs explicit per-instance parameterization or descriptor evolution before graph edges can drive dependency calls.

product_parameterization_gaps.evidence: http-active-router is seeded as a descriptor/image product, but #870 has not yet proven the exact edge-to-target runtime parameter path.
product_parameterization_gaps.impact: Router scenarios must not hard-code local script variables outside graph-derived material.

product_parameterization_gaps.evidence: Descriptor exposes primary required and observer-a/observer-b optional HTTP requirement sockets with env bindings.
product_parameterization_gaps.impact: Binding synthesis must handle required and optional requirements differently.

product_parameterization_gaps.evidence: postgres-server descriptor declares POSTGRES_PASSWORD as secret delivery and postgres-data as retained data.
product_parameterization_gaps.impact: Docker interpreter needs a secret resolver and ownership-retained volume model; no password may enter durable descriptors or logs.

product_parameterization_gaps.gap: Published image digest truth versus local tags
product_parameterization_gaps.evidence: seed descriptors pin GHCR/Docker Hub digests and published-image smoke scripts disable local rebuilds.
product_parameterization_gaps.impact: Final ACTIVITY acceptance must use immutable digest pulls; cpk-server backend changes require republish and descriptor/catalogue checksum updates.

topology_decision.reason: The inserted approval queue issues #880 and #881 remain correctly positioned before public cpk-server route acceptance. The next needed change is not another topology issue; it is the #871 adapter boundary that carries pinned realization material into the existing coordinator seam.
handoff_to_871: Keep ExecutionCoordinator as the single durable coordinator.
Do not introduce a second effect language or scheduler.

Define a realization boundary richer than PlannedActivity alone, including run/workspace identity, admitted plan truth, desired/current graph material, registered product documents, and bounded runtime configuration evidence.
The adapter still executes only after durable intent commits and returns ActivityExecutionOutcome.

non_goals: CPK wrapper routes inside arbitrary product servers
